title: Remotely Validating XML Streams
text: Remotely Validating XML Streams You can use XML streams to pass data to another component for validation. This process is known as remote validation. The benefit of remote validation is that validation logic can be centralized and located in dedicated validation components. To remotely validate an XML stream: Send the data as an XML stream to the validation component. The XML stream must include the id , crc , status and valerr attributes for all entity elements in the XML stream, and include the valerr attribute for all field elements. Load the data from the XML stream into the validation component, and reconnect the data to component and database data, using retrieve/reconnect . Validate the data. If necessary, modify the error triggers to set values for the errormsg property of $occproperties and $fieldproperties . Exit the error trigger with a negative value, for example return -1 . Send the data back to the initial component as an XML stream using the id , crc , status , and valerr attributes. Values for errormsg are placed in the valerr attribute by the xmlsave statement. Load the data from the XML stream into the initial component. Each valerr attribute encountered by xmlload fires an occurrence-level or field-level error trigger, with $error is 0161. Related Links Attributes in Uniface XML Streams Uniface uses attributes in XML streams to store data processing information and to define custom attributes. Uniface XML Stream Processing Uniface uses the xmlsave and xmlload ProcScript statements to transfer and convert data (including validation and modification state) between Uniface component and XML streams. Attributes determine the state of data in an XML stream. These are defined in the DTD for the XML stream. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
